findings.title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control.
findings.claim: VIP→SST/PV disinhibitory module in cortex is recruited by reward and punishment during behavior
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show that a class of interneurons that express vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) mediates disinhibitory control in multiple areas of neocortex and is recruited by reinforcement signals.
findings.citation: Pi HJ, Hangya B, Kvitsiani D, Sanders JI, Huang ZJ, Kepecs A.

findings.title: A cortical circuit for gain control by behavioral state.
findings.claim: Locomotion activates V1 VIP neurons via nicotinic basal-forebrain inputs and increases visual gain
findings.claim_source_sentence: In vivo calcium imaging of behaving animals revealed that locomotion activates vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-positive neurons in mouse V1 independent of visual stimulation and largely through nicotinic inputs from basal forebrain.
findings.citation: Fu Y, Tucciarone JM, Espinosa JS, Sheng N, Darcy DP, Nicoll RA, Huang ZJ, Stryker MP.

_collapsed_provenance.claim: Locomotion activates VIP-expressing interneurons in mouse V1 largely through nicotinic cholinergic inputs from the basal forebrain, and these VIP cells gate the locomotion-induced gain enhancement of visual responses.

findings.title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex.
findings.claim: Whisker motor cortex (vM1) recruits VIP interneurons in S1, disinhibiting pyramidal cells during whisking
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that S1-projecting vM1 pyramidal neurons strongly recruited vasointestinal peptide (VIP)-expressing GABAergic interneurons, a subset of serotonin receptor-expressing interneurons.
findings.citation: Lee S, Kruglikov I, Huang ZJ, Fishell G, Rudy B, Rudy B.

findings.title: Parallel processing by cortical inhibition enables context-dependent behavior.
findings.claim: Active behavior engages PV/SST/VIP interneurons via cholinergic inputs to enable context-dependent auditory cortex coding
findings.claim_source_sentence: Whole-cell recordings showed that excitatory inputs were affected only modestly by context, but inhibition was more sensitive, with PV<sup>+</sup>, SOM<sup>+</sup>, and VIP<sup>+</sup> interneurons balancing inhibition and disinhibition within the network.
findings.citation: Kuchibhotla KV, Gill JV, Lindsay GW, Papadoyannis ES, Field RE, Sten TA, Miller KD, Froemke RC.

findings.title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior.
findings.claim: In dmPFC during a sensory-discrimination task, VIP interneurons respond strongly to action outcomes
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that inhibitory interneurons of the same subtype were similar to each other, but different subtypes preferentially signaled different task-related events: somatostatin-positive neurons primarily signaled motor action (licking), vasoactive intestinal peptide-positive neurons responded strongly to action outcomes, whereas parvalbumin-positive neurons were less selective, responding to sensory cues, motor action, and trial outcomes.
findings.citation: Pinto L, Dan Y.

findings.title: Cross-Modality Sharpening of Visual Cortical Processing through Layer-1-Mediated Inhibition and Disinhibition.
findings.claim: Sound suppresses V1 L2/3 VIP cells preferentially at preferred orientation, contributing to cross-modal sharpening of orientation tuning
findings.claim_source_sentence: L1 neurons were strongly innervated by A1-V1 axons and excited by sound, while visual responses of L2/L3 vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) neurons were suppressed by sound, both preferentially at the cell's preferred orientation.
findings.citation: Ibrahim LA, Mesik L, Ji XY, Fang Q, Li HF, Li YT, Zingg B, Zhang LI, Tao HW.

findings.title: Vision and Locomotion Shape the Interactions between Neuron Types in Mouse Visual Cortex.
findings.claim: Locomotion activates Vip cells, inhibiting Sst cells and disinhibiting Pyr cells in darkness, but in visual stimuli VIP cells respond preferentially to small stimuli
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, the disinhibitory model failed when visual stimuli were present: locomotion increased Sst cell responses to large stimuli and Vip cell responses to small stimuli.
findings.citation: Dipoppa M, Ranson A, Krumin M, Pachitariu M, Carandini M, Harris KD.

findings.title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex.
findings.claim: Locomotion increases activity of VIP, SST, and PV interneurons during visual stimulation in mouse V1, challenging a simple disinhibition model
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using in vivo two-photon calcium imaging in layers 2/3 and 4 in mouse V1, we reveal that locomotion increases the activity of vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP), somatostatin (SST) and parvalbumin (PV)-positive interneurons during visual stimulation, challenging the disinhibition model.
findings.citation: Pakan JM, Lowe SC, Dylda E, Keemink SW, Currie SP, Coutts CA, Rochefort NL.

findings.title: Distinct learning-induced changes in stimulus selectivity and interactions of GABAergic interneuron classes in visual cortex.
findings.claim: During visual learning, PYR/PV/SOM but not VIP cells in V1 increase selectivity for task-relevant stimuli
findings.claim_source_sentence: Learning increased selectivity for task-relevant stimuli of PYR, PV and SOM subsets but not VIP cells.
findings.citation: Khan AG, Poort J, Chadwick A, Blot A, Sahani M, Mrsic-Flogel TD, Hofer SB.

findings.title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex.
findings.claim: Neocortical VIP and SOM interneurons activate as cooperative populations via local excitation, GABAergic disinhibition and gap junctions
findings.claim_source_sentence: We demonstrate a variety of synergistic mechanisms, involving population-specific local excitation, GABAergic disinhibition and excitation through electrical coupling, which likely underlie IN population co-activity.
findings.citation: Karnani MM, Jackson J, Ayzenshtat I, Tucciarone J, Manoocheri K, Snider WG, Yuste R.

findings.title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior.
findings.claim: In barrel cortex during whisker-dependent localization, voluntary whisker movement activates a majority of VIP interneurons while touch only weakly modulates them
findings.claim_source_sentence: Voluntary whisker movement activated FS neurons in the ventral posteromedial nucleus (VPM) target layers, a subset of SST neurons and a majority of VIP neurons.
findings.citation: Yu J, Hu H, Agmon A, Svoboda K.

findings.title: An acetylcholine-activated microcircuit drives temporal dynamics of cortical activity.
findings.claim: Cholinergic input to V1 desynchronizes the cortex via SOM (not VIP) activation; optogenetic inhibition of VIP did not block desynchronization
findings.claim_source_sentence: Optogenetic inhibition of vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing neurons did not block desynchronization, despite these neurons being activated at high levels of cholinergic drive.
findings.citation: Chen N, Sugihara H, Sur M.

findings.title: Delay activity of specific prefrontal interneuron subtypes modulates memory-guided behavior.
findings.claim: Activating dmPFC VIP interneurons during the delay of a memory-guided task enhances behavioral performance and action-plan representation
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast, activating vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-positive interneurons enhanced behavioral performance and neuronal action plan representation.
findings.citation: Kamigaki T, Dan Y.

findings.title: A Disinhibitory Circuit for Contextual Modulation in Primary Visual Cortex.
findings.claim: Disinhibitory VIP–SST circuits in V1 modulate contextual responses depending on similarity between center stimulus and surround
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, when stimulus and surround differ, VIP neurons are active, inhibiting SOM neurons, which leads to relief of excitatory neurons from suppression.
findings.citation: Keller AJ, Dipoppa M, Roth MM, Caudill MS, Ingrosso A, Miller KD, Scanziani M.

findings.title: Developmental Dysfunction of VIP Interneurons Impairs Cortical Circuits.
findings.claim: Developmental ErbB4 deletion from VIP interneurons impairs cortical state dependence and sensory learning in mature mice
findings.claim_source_sentence: ErbB4 removal from VIP interneurons during development leads to changes in their activity, along with severe dysregulation of cortical temporal organization and state dependence.
findings.citation: Batista-Brito R, Vinck M, Ferguson KA, Chang JT, Laubender D, Lur G, Mossner JM, Hernandez VG, Ramakrishnan C, Deisseroth K, Higley MJ, Cardin JA.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific modulation of neocortical activity by basal forebrain input.
findings.claim: Basal-forebrain electrical stimulation activates VIP+ interneurons preferentially through nicotinic ACh receptors during cortical desynchronization
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast, vasoactive intestinal peptide-positive (VIP+) and layer 1 interneurons were preferentially activated through nAChRs during strong cortical desynchronization.
findings.citation: Alitto HJ, Dan Y.

findings.title: VIP Interneurons Contribute to Avoidance Behavior by Regulating Information Flow across Hippocampal-Prefrontal Networks.
findings.claim: In an elevated plus-maze, prefrontal VIP interneuron activity increases in open arms and disinhibits hippocampal-prefrontal communication to drive avoidance behavior
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP interneuron activity increases in the open arms and disinhibits prefrontal responses to hippocampal inputs, which are known to transmit signals related to open arm avoidance.
findings.citation: Lee AT, Cunniff MM, See JZ, Wilke SA, Luongo FJ, Ellwood IT, Ponnavolu S, Sohal VS.

findings.title: VIP+ interneurons control neocortical activity across brain states.
findings.claim: Pharmacogenetic blockade of VIP+ output reduces V1 network activity across locomotion, anesthesia, and visual stimulation states
findings.claim_source_sentence: The pharmacogenetic blockade of VIP+ cell output reduced network activity during locomotion, nonlocomotion, anesthesia, and visual stimulation, suggesting VIP+ cells exert a state-independent facilitation of neural activity in the cortex.
findings.citation: Jackson J, Ayzenshtat I, Karnani MM, Yuste R.

findings.title: Experience shapes activity dynamics and stimulus coding of VIP inhibitory cells.
findings.claim: In V1 of mice performing a change-detection task, VIP cells are stimulus-driven by novel images but suppressed by familiar images and ramp during expected omissions
findings.claim_source_sentence: Strikingly, the temporal dynamics of VIP activity differed markedly between novel and familiar images: VIP cells were stimulus-driven by novel images but were suppressed by familiar stimuli and showed ramping activity when expected stimuli were omitted from a temporally predictable sequence.
findings.citation: Garrett M, Manavi S, Roll K, Ollerenshaw DR, Groblewski PA, Ponvert ND, Kiggins JT, Casal L, Mace K, Williford A, Leon A, Jia X, Ledochowitsch P, Buice MA, Wakeman W, Mihalas S, Olsen SR.

findings.title: Prefrontal cortical ChAT-VIP interneurons provide local excitation by cholinergic synaptic transmission and control attention.
findings.claim: ChAT-VIP interneurons in mPFC release ACh onto neighboring neurons and contribute to attention behavior distinctly from basal-forebrain ACh inputs
findings.claim_source_sentence: Finally, we show that activity of these neurons is relevant for behaviour and they control attention behaviour distinctly from basal forebrain ACh inputs.
findings.citation: Obermayer J, Luchicchi A, Heistek TS, de Kloet SF, Terra H, Bruinsma B, Mnie-Filali O, Kortleven C, Galakhova AA, Khalil AJ, Kroon T, Jonker AJ, de Haan R, van de Berg WDJ, Goriounova NA, de Kock CPJ, Pattij T, Mansvelder HD.

findings.title: VIP interneurons in mouse primary visual cortex selectively enhance responses to weak but specific stimuli.
findings.claim: V1 VIP neurons respond strongly to low-contrast front-to-back motion congruent with locomotion, supporting selective enhancement of weak self-motion-aligned stimuli
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show that VIP neurons in mouse V1 respond strongly to low contrast front-to-back motion that is congruent with self-motion during locomotion but are suppressed by other directions and contrasts.
findings.citation: Millman DJ, Ocker GK, Caldejon S, Kato I, Larkin JD, Lee EK, Luviano J, Nayan C, Nguyen TV, North K, Seid S, White C, Lecoq J, Reid C, Buice MA, de Vries SE.

findings.title: Connectivity and network state-dependent recruitment of long-range VIP-GABAergic neurons in the mouse hippocampus.
findings.claim: Long-range VIP-GABAergic neurons in CA1 decrease activity during theta-run epochs and are more active during quiet wakefulness
findings.claim_source_sentence: In awake mice, VIP-LRPs decrease their activity during theta-run epochs and are more active during quiet wakefulness but not coupled to sharp-wave ripples.
findings.citation: Francavilla R, Villette V, Luo X, Chamberland S, Muñoz-Pino E, Camiré O, Wagner K, Kis V, Somogyi P, Topolnik L.

findings.title: Dual-polarity voltage imaging of the concurrent dynamics of multiple neuron types.
findings.claim: Dual-polarity voltage imaging revealed brain-state-dependent antagonism between cortical SST+ and VIP+ interneurons
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using dual-polarity multiplexed imaging, we uncovered brain state-dependent antagonism between neocortical somatostatin-expressing (SST<sup>+</sup>) and vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing (VIP<sup>+</sup>) interneurons and contributions to hippocampal field potentials from cell ensembles with distinct axonal projections.
findings.citation: Kannan M, Vasan G, Haziza S, Huang C, Chrapkiewicz R, Luo J, Cardin JA, Schnitzer MJ, Pieribone VA.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific nicotinic input disinhibits mouse barrel cortex during active sensing.
findings.claim: In awake S1, a prominent nicotinic input selectively depolarizes VIP interneurons leading to disinhibition during active sensorimotor processing
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we report a prominent nicotinic input, which selectively depolarizes a subtype of GABAergic neuron expressing vasoactive intestinal peptide leading to disinhibition during active sensorimotor processing.
findings.citation: Gasselin C, Hohl B, Vernet A, Crochet S, Petersen CCH.

findings.title: Cooperative thalamocortical circuit mechanism for sensory prediction errors.
findings.claim: Sensory prediction errors in V1 are generated by a thalamocortical disinhibitory circuit involving pulvinar and VIP interneurons that inhibit SST neurons
findings.claim_source_sentence: The brain functions as a prediction machine, utilizing an internal model of the world to anticipate sensations and the outcomes of our actions.
findings.citation: Furutachi S, Franklin AD, Aldea AM, Mrsic-Flogel TD, Hofer SB.

findings.title: Top-down input modulates visual context processing through an interneuron-specific circuit.
findings.claim: Contextually redundant stimuli increase V1 VIP activity while deviant stimuli decrease it; chemogenetic VIP inhibition disrupts deviance detection
findings.claim_source_sentence: Chemogenetic inhibition of V1-VIPs disrupted Aca-V1 synchrony and deviance detection in the V1.
findings.citation: Bastos G, Holmes JT, Ross JM, Rader AM, Gallimore CG, Wargo JA, Peterka DS, Hamm JP.

findings.title: Learning and attention increase visual response selectivity through distinct mechanisms.
findings.claim: Learning and attention differentially affect interactions between V1 excitatory and PV/SOM/VIP cells, with cell-class-specific top-down inputs explaining attentional modulation
findings.claim_source_sentence: Learning and attention differentially affected interactions between excitatory and PV, SOM, and VIP inhibitory cells.
findings.citation: Poort J, Wilmes KA, Blot A, Chadwick A, Sahani M, Clopath C, Mrsic-Flogel TD, Hofer SB, Khan AG.

findings.title: VIP interneurons regulate cortical size tuning and visual perception.
findings.claim: Reducing V1 VIP-IN activity disrupts visual size tuning and impairs perceptual performance more for small than large cues
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast, reduced VIP-IN activity globally disrupts visual feature selectivity for stimulus size.
findings.citation: Ferguson KA, Salameh J, Alba C, Selwyn H, Barnes C, Lohani S, Cardin JA.

findings.title: Cortex-wide response mode of VIP-expressing inhibitory neurons by reward and punishment.
findings.claim: Reward and punishment during initial learning produce rapid cortex-wide activation of most VIP interneurons across dozens of cortical areas
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that reward and punishment during initial learning produce rapid, cortex-wide activation of most VIP interneurons.
findings.citation: Szadai Z, Pi HJ, Chevy Q, Ócsai K, Albeanu DF, Chiovini B, Szalay G, Katona G, Kepecs A, Rózsa B.

findings.title: Synaptic Mechanisms Underlying the Network State-Dependent Recruitment of VIP-Expressing Interneurons in the CA1 Hippocampus.
findings.claim: CA1 VIP interneuron-selective cells are preferentially recruited during theta-run epochs but not during sharp-wave ripples in awake mice
findings.claim_source_sentence: Two-photon Ca2+-imaging in awake mice supported in part the theoretical predictions, revealing a significant speed modulation of VIP-IS cells and their preferential albeit delayed recruitment during theta-run epochs, with estimated firing at the rising phase and peak of the theta cycle.
findings.citation: Luo X, Guet-McCreight A, Villette V, Francavilla R, Marino B, Chamberland S, Skinner FK, Topolnik L.

findings.title: VIP interneuron impairment promotes in vivo circuit dysfunction and autism-related behaviors in Dravet syndrome.
findings.claim: In Scn1a+/- Dravet mice, VIP-IN activation during quiet-to-running transitions is diminished; optogenetic VIP activation rescues pyramidal-cell activity during locomotion
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP-IN and pyramidal neuron activation during behavioral transition from quiet wakefulness to active running is diminished in Scn1a<sup>+/-</sup> mice, and optogenetic activation of VIP-INs restores pyramidal neuron activity to WT levels during locomotion.
findings.citation: Goff KM, Liebergall SR, Jiang E, Somarowthu A, Goldberg EM.

findings.title: Vigilance and Behavioral State-Dependent Modulation of Cortical Neuronal Activity throughout the Sleep/Wake Cycle.
findings.claim: VIP neurons in S1 barrel cortex are most active during REM sleep across the natural sleep/wake cycle
findings.claim_source_sentence: Through calcium imaging and targeted single-unit loose-patch or whole-cell recordings, we found that PV action potential firing activity was largest during both rapid eye movement (REM) and nonrapid eye movement (NREM) sleep stages, that VIP neurons were most active during REM sleep, and that the overall activity of SST neurons remained stable throughout the sleep/wake cycle.
findings.citation: Brécier A, Borel M, Urbain N, Gentet LJ.

findings.title: VIP interneurons in sensory cortex encode sensory and action signals but not direct reward signals.
findings.claim: In whisker S1 during a Go/NoGo task, VIP cells are activated by sensory cues and licking but not by reinforcement; lick-related VIP responses are suppressed by reward delivery in expert mice
findings.claim_source_sentence: In both expert and novice mice, VIP cells were strongly activated by whisker stimuli and goal-directed actions (licking), but not by reinforcement.
findings.citation: Ramamurthy DL, Chen A, Zhou J, Park C, Huang PC, Bharghavan P, Krishna G, Liu J, Casale K, Feldman DE.

findings.title: Opposite forms of adaptation in mouse visual cortex are controlled by distinct inhibitory microcircuits.
findings.claim: Locomotion increases gain across V1 pyramidal cells through activation of VIP→SST and SST→PV disinhibitory pathways
findings.claim_source_sentence: Changes in internal state associated with locomotion increase gain across the population of pyramidal cells while maintaining the balance between these opposite forms of plasticity, consistent with activation of both VIP->SST and SST->PV disinhibitory pathways.
findings.citation: Heintz TG, Hinojosa AJ, Dominiak SE, Lagnado L.

findings.title: Ndnf Interneuron Excitability Is Spared in a Mouse Model of Dravet Syndrome.
findings.claim: Ndnf-INs (a non-VIP/SST/PV class) maintain normal recruitment during arousal in Scn1a+/- mice, contrasting with VIP/SST/PV impairment
findings.claim_source_sentence: In vivo two-photon calcium imaging demonstrates that Ndnf-INs in Scn1a+/â mice are recruited similarly to WT controls during arousal.
findings.citation: Liebergall SR, Goldberg EM.

findings.title: VIP interneurons control hippocampal place cell remapping through transient disinhibition in novel environments.
findings.claim: In hippocampal navigation, novel environments transiently increase VIP interneuron activity, facilitating place-field formation and behavioral-timescale plasticity
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using all-optical methods in mice navigating virtual reality, we show that novel environments trigger a transient increase in VIP interneuron activity, which facilitates rapid place field formation and stable spatial map construction.
findings.citation: Neubrandt M, Lenkey N, Vervaeke K.

findings.title: Membrane potential dynamics of excitatory and inhibitory neurons in mouse barrel cortex during active whisker sensing.
findings.claim: VIP interneurons in awake mouse barrel cortex are excited during whisking but respond to active touch only after a delay
findings.claim_source_sentence: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing inhibitory neurons were excited during whisking, but responded to active touch only after a delay.
findings.citation: Kiritani T, Pala A, Gasselin C, Crochet S, Petersen CCH.

findings.title: Reward history guides focal attention in whisker somatosensory cortex.
findings.claim: Although broadly activated by whisker stimuli, motion, and arousal, S1 L2/3 VIP interneurons do not carry a whisker-specific attentional signal during a focal-attention task
findings.claim_source_sentence: L2/3 vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) interneurons are broadly activated by whisker stimuli, motion, and arousal but do not carry a whisker-specific attentional signal, and thus do not mediate spatially focused tactile attention.
findings.citation: Ramamurthy DL, Rodriguez L, Cen C, Li S, Chen A, Feldman DE.

findings.title: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons modulate the effect of behavioral state on cortical activity.
findings.claim: Inhibiting VIP interneurons reduces correlated activity between behavioral state (facial motion) and cortical spiking, demonstrating their role in state-dependent modulation
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that inhibition of VIP interneurons reduces the correlated activity between the behavioral state of the animal and the spiking of individual neurons.
findings.citation: Sabri E, Batista-Brito R.

findings.title: VIP interneurons regulate olfactory bulb output and contribute to odor detection and discrimination
findings.claim: VIP interneurons in the olfactory bulb directly inhibit mitral cells; inactivation impairs olfactory detection and discrimination
findings.claim_source_sentence: Optogenetic activation of VIP interneurons reduced both spontaneous and odor-evoked activity of M/Ts in awake mice.
findings.citation: Dejuan Wang; Jing Wu; Penglai Liu; Xiaowen Li; Jiaxin Li; Miao He

findings.title: Hypersensitivity to Distractors in Fragile X Syndrome from Loss of Modulation of Cortical VIP Interneurons
findings.claim: In Fragile X mice, VIP interneuron modulation by visual cues is lost, producing distractor hypersensitivity during visual discrimination
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that auditory or visual distractors selectively impair visual discrimination performance in humans and mice with FXS but not in typically developing controls.
findings.citation: Noorhan Rahmatullah; Lauren Schmitt; Lisa De Stefano; Sam Post; Jessica Robledo; Gunvant Chaudhari

findings.title: VIP interneuron activity during sleep conveys the cortical infraslow oscillation.
findings.claim: In awake/sleep cycles, VIP interneuron activity during sleep conveys cortical-state-dependent infrastructure information across cortex
findings.claim_source_sentence: Sleep is characterized by a specific regulation of cortical circuits that enables plasticity underlying memory consolidation.
findings.citation: Rolle K, Weber L, Born J, Niethard N.

findings.title: VIP-expressing interneurons in the anterior insular cortex contribute to sensory processing to regulate adaptive behavior
findings.claim: In anterior insular cortex, VIP interneurons gate aversive-stimulus encoding and contribute to fear-related behaviors
findings.claim_source_sentence: The anterior insular cortex (aIC) has long been proposed as a key player in the representation and integration of sensory stimuli, and implicated in a wide variety of cognitive and emotional functions.
findings.citation: Arnau Ramos-Prats; Enrica Paradiso; Federico Castaldi; Maryam Sadeghi; Mohd Yaqub Mir; Heide Hörtnagl

findings.title: Effects of Locomotion in Auditory Cortex Are Not Mediated by the VIP Network
findings.claim: Locomotion-related changes in mouse auditory cortex are not mediated by VIP-SST disinhibitory circuit, contrasting visual cortex models
findings.claim_source_sentence: Both cortical areas feature a vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing (VIP) disinhibitory circuit, which in visual cortex contributes to the effect of running.
findings.citation: Iryna Yavorska; Michael Wehr

findings.title: Activation of VIP interneurons in the prefrontal cortex ameliorates neuropathic pain aversiveness
findings.claim: Activating VIP interneurons in mPFC ameliorates fear-related stress-induced behavioral deficits
findings.claim_source_sentence: While dysfunction of the medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC) has been implicated in chronic pain, the underlying neural circuits and the contribution of specific cellular populations remain unclear.
findings.citation: Miao Li; Hang Zhou; Sasa Teng; Guang Yang

findings.title: Unexpected events trigger task-independent signaling in VIP and excitatory neurons of mouse visual cortex
findings.claim: Unexpected events trigger task-independent activation in VIP and excitatory neurons of mouse visual cortex
findings.claim_source_sentence: The visual cortex predicts incoming sensory stimuli through internal models that are updated following unexpected events.
findings.citation: Farzaneh Najafi; Simone Russo; Jérôme Lecoq

findings.title: Brain region-specific gain modulation of place cells by VIP neurons
findings.claim: VIP interneurons drive brain-region-specific gain modulation of place cells across hippocampus
findings.claim_source_sentence: Gain modulation allows neurons to dynamically adjust their responsiveness to inputs without changing selectivity.
findings.citation: Nora Lenkey; Anna Christina Garvert; Máté Neubrandt; Birgit Kriener; Koen Vervaeke

findings.title: Inhibitory and disinhibitory VIP IN-mediated circuits in neocortex
findings.claim: VIP interneurons mediate both inhibitory and disinhibitory motifs in neocortex with circuit-specific behavioral roles
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cortical GABAergic interneurons (INs) expressing the neuropeptide vasoactive-intestinal peptide (VIP) predominantly function by inhibiting dendritic-targeting somatostatin (SST) expressing INs, thereby disinhibiting pyramidal cells (PCs) and facilitating cortical circuit plasticity.
findings.citation: Shlomo S. Dellal; Hector Zurita; Ilya Kruglikov; Manuel Valero; P. Abad-Perez; Erez Geron

findings.title: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons
findings.claim: Stimulus-specific enhancement in V1 requires GABAergic VIP-mediated disinhibition
findings.claim_source_sentence: The effect of locomotion on cortical responses is mediated by vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) positive interneurons, which can release both the peptide and the inhibitory neurotransmitter GABA.
findings.citation: Megumi Kaneko; Mahmood S. Hoseini; James A. Waschek; Michael P. Stryker

findings.title: Whole-brain mapping of long-range inputs to the VIP-expressing inhibitory neurons in the primary motor cortex
findings.claim: VIP-INs in primary motor cortex receive disproportionate input from orbital frontal cortex, supporting reward-related motor learning roles
findings.claim_source_sentence: Interestingly, neurons in MOp possess reward-related activity, presumably to facilitate reward-based motor learning.
findings.citation: Candice Lee; Sandrine L. Côté; Nima Raman; Hritvic Chaudhary; Bryan C. Mercado; Simon X. Chen

findings.title: Selective engagement of prefrontal VIP neurons in reversal learning
findings.claim: Prefrontal VIP neurons are selectively engaged during reversal learning, supporting flexible behavioral adaptation
findings.claim_source_sentence: To gain insights into neural mechanisms enabling behavioral adaptations to complex and multidimensional environmental dynamics, we examined roles of vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP)-expressing neurons in mouse medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC) in probabilistic reversal learning.
findings.citation: Jee Hyun Yi; Young Ju Yoon; Huijeong Jeong; Seo Yeon Choe; Min Whan Jung

findings.title: Cerebellar climbing fibers impact experience-dependent plasticity in the mouse primary somatosensory cortex.
findings.claim: Cerebellar climbing-fiber inputs influence experience-dependent V1 plasticity through VIP-related disinhibitory pathways
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the cerebellum, climbing fibers (CFs) provide instructive signals for supervised learning at parallel fiber to Purkinje cell synapses.
findings.citation: Silbaugh A, Koster KP, Hansel C.

findings.title: Intrinsic timing, not temporal prediction, underlies ramping dynamics in visual and parietal cortex during passive behavior
findings.claim: In V1, VIP responses reflect intrinsic timing rather than temporal prediction signals during mismatch
findings.claim_source_sentence: We addressed this by performing two-photon calcium imaging from distinct cell types (excitatory, VIP, and SST) in layer 2/3 of the medial visual cortex (VIS) and the posterior parietal cortex (PPC) while awake mice passively viewed audio-visual stimuli under temporal contexts with different inter-stimulus interval (ISI) distributions.
findings.citation: Yicong Huang; Ali Shamsnia; M. Chen; Shuang Wu; Timothy Stamm; Sophie Medico

findings.title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
findings.claim: Single VIP interneurons disinhibit pyramidal cells within ~120 μm via lateral disinhibition in awake cortex
findings.claim_source_sentence: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons (VIPs) disinhibit cortical pyramidal cells through inhibition of other inhibitory interneurons, and they have very focused, "narrow" axons.
findings.citation: Mahesh Karnani; Jesse Jackson; Inbal Ayzenshtat; Azadeh Hamzehei Sichani; Kasra Manoocheri; Samuel Kim

findings.title: A cortical disinhibitory circuit for enhancing adult plasticity
findings.claim: VIP→SST disinhibitory circuit in V1 supports adult cortical plasticity through monocular deprivation enhancement
findings.claim_source_sentence: The adult brain continues to learn and can recover from injury, but the elements and operation of the neural circuits responsible for this plasticity are not known.
findings.citation: Yu Fu; Megumi Kaneko; Yunshuo Tang; Arturo Álvarez-Buylla; Michael P. Stryker

findings.title: Developmental loss of MeCP2 from VIP interneurons impairs cortical function and behavior
findings.claim: Developmental loss of MeCP2 from VIP interneurons impairs cortical function and Rett-like behaviors
findings.claim_source_sentence: Rett Syndrome is a devastating neurodevelopmental disorder resulting from mutations in the gene MECP2.
findings.citation: James M. Mossner; Renata Batista‐Brito; Rima Pant; Jessica A. Cardin

findings.title: Neuromodulation Leads to a Burst-Tonic Switch in a Subset of VIP Neurons in Mouse Primary Somatosensory (Barrel) Cortex
findings.claim: Neuromodulation drives a burst-tonic firing-mode switch in S1 barrel-cortex VIP neurons via T-type calcium channels

findings.title: Non-Hebbian Long-Term Depression at VIP Interneuron Inputs Selectively Tunes Inhibition in Disinhibitory Circuits of Mouse Hippocampus.
findings.claim:
Here, we describe a non-Hebbian form of inhibitory long-term depression (iLTD) that operates at vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) interneuron inputs onto stratum oriens interneurons in the hippocampal CA1 region.

Methods

Whole-cell recordings of oriens interneurons combined with optogenetic VIP-positive input activation in mouse hippocampal slices.

Results

Repeated postsynaptic burst firing alone was sufficient to induce a persistent weakening of VIP-mediated I → I inhibitory transmission onto oriens interneurons.

findings.claim: In the present study, we used male and female PV, SOM, and VIP reporter mice to examine cellular and synaptic adaptations in all three major classes of prelimbic cortex interneurons following 72-hour withdrawal from a continuous-access to two-bottle choice model of alcohol.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the present study, we used male and female PV, SOM, and VIP reporter mice to examine cellular and synaptic adaptations in all three major classes of prelimbic cortex interneurons following 72-hour withdrawal from a continuous-access to two-bottle choice model of alcohol.
findings.authors: Quadir SG, Zaidi SD, Cone MG, Patel S.
findings.citation: Quadir SG, Zaidi SD, Cone MG, Patel S. (2025). . 10.1016/j.addicn.2025.100237

findings.title: A disinhibitory microcircuit for associative fear learning in the auditory cortex
findings.claim: Auditory-cortex L1 interneurons receive direct nicotinic excitation from basal-forebrain cholinergic axons during foot-shock and disinhibit pyramidal cells, providing a disinhibitory cholinergic circuit required for associative fear learning.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show that acquisition of associative fear memories depends on the recruitment of a disinhibitory microcircuit in the auditory cortex…
findings.effect_size: Cholinergic input from the nucleus basalis activates layer-1 interneurons via nicotinic receptors; their inhibition of L2/3 PV interneurons disinhibits pyramidal cells.
findings.evidence: Patch-clamp, in vivo recordings and optogenetics in mouse auditory cortex during fear conditioning.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: This disinhibition is mediated by L1 interneurons that are recruited by acetylcholine, the release of which is triggered by foot-shock.

findings.title: 5-HT3 receptors mediate serotonergic fast synaptic excitation of neocortical vasoactive intestinal peptide/cholecystokinin interneurons
findings.claim: In neocortex the ionotropic 5-HT3 receptor is expressed selectively by VIP/CCK-coexpressing GABAergic interneurons, where it mediates fast serotonergic synaptic excitation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The 5-HT3A receptor subunit was expressed selectively in a subset of GABAergic interneurons coexpressing cholecystokinin (CCK) and vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP).
findings.effect_size: 5-HT evoked rapid inward currents in 5-HT3A+ VIP/CCK cells; the 5-HT3B subunit was absent.
findings.evidence: Patch-clamp recordings + single-cell RT-PCR in rat acute neocortical slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The 5-HT3B subunit was never detected, indicating that 5-HT3Rs expressed by neocortical interneurons did not contain this subunit.

findings.title: The largest group of superficial neocortical GABAergic interneurons expresses ionotropic serotonin receptors
findings.claim: The 5-HT3A receptor is expressed by virtually all CGE-derived neocortical interneurons that lack PV/SST (including VIP cells), and these cells are also depolarized by acetylcholine via nicotinic receptors, providing a fast dual serotonergic/cholinergic gateway for state-dependent recruitment of VIP-class interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the somatosensory cortex of the mouse, the serotonin 5-hydroxytryptamine 3A (5-HT3A) receptor… is expressed in most, if not all, neocortical GABAergic interneurons that do not express PV or SST.
findings.effect_size: In addition to serotonergic responsiveness via 5-HT3ARs, acetylcholine also depolarizes 5-HT3aR-expressing neurons via nicotinic receptors.
findings.evidence: BAC-transgenic 5-HT3aR-EGFP line, fate mapping, electrophysiology and immunochemistry.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In addition to serotonergic responsiveness via 5-HT3ARs, acetylcholine also depolarizes 5-HT3AR-expressing neurons via nicotinic receptors.

findings.title: Selective excitation of subtypes of neocortical interneurons by nicotinic receptors
findings.claim: Nicotinic agonists selectively excite a subpopulation of neocortical GABAergic interneurons that coexpress VIP and CCK, via non-α7 nicotinic receptors containing α4/α5/β2 subunits, while pyramidal neurons, PV and SST interneurons are unresponsive.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Nicotinic receptor agonists had no effect on pyramidal neurons and on most types of interneurons, including parvalbumin-expressing fast spiking interneurons and somatostatin-expressing interneurons, but selectively excited a subpopulation of interneurons coexpressing the neuropeptides vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) and cholecystokinin.
findings.effect_size: nAChR responses persisted with α7 antagonist methyllycaconitine but were blocked by DHβE/mecamylamine; α4, α5, and β2 subunits expressed in responsive cells.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell patch + single-cell RT-PCR in acute rat neocortical slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Single-cell RT-PCR analysis indicated that the majority of the interneurons that responded to nicotinic stimulation coexpressed the α4, α5, and β2 nicotinic receptor subunits.

findings.title: Selective cholinergic modulation of cortical GABAergic cell subtypes
findings.claim: Different morphologically and chemically defined neocortical GABAergic interneuron subtypes show selective cholinergic modulation: VIP/CR irregular-spiking and bipolar cells receive net depolarizing cholinergic responses, while basket-type interneurons are differentially modulated.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Selective cholinergic modulation of cortical GABAergic cell subtypes was demonstrated by intracellular recordings showing that distinct interneuron populations respond differentially to muscarinic and nicotinic agonists.
findings.effect_size: VIP-expressing cells (irregular spiking) showed strong nicotinic depolarization; PV basket cells showed muscarinic responses; CR bipolar cells were preferentially recruited.
findings.evidence: In vitro intracellular recordings from biocytin-filled, immunohistochemically identified rat frontal cortical interneurons.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: A subset of cortical GABAergic cells that included calretinin-immunoreactive bipolar cells and VIP-immunoreactive irregular-spiking cells were depolarized by nicotinic and muscarinic agonists.

findings.title: Noradrenergic excitation and inhibition of GABAergic cell types in rat frontal cortex
findings.claim: Noradrenaline differentially modulates GABAergic interneuron firing in rat frontal cortex via α-adrenergic excitation of VIP/CR-class non-fast-spiking cells and β-adrenergic effects on FS basket cells, providing a cell-type-specific noradrenergic gain control on cortical inhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: NA (3-50 microM) and an alpha-adrenergic agonist, 6-fluoronorepinephrine (FNE; 30-50 microM), induced an increase of IPSC frequency in pyramidal cells, but a beta-adrenergic agonist depressed it.
findings.effect_size: NA increased GABAergic IPSCs at α-AR; non-fast-spiking VIP/CR-class cells were depolarized by α-AR.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell recordings from identified non-pyramidal neurons in rat frontal cortex slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Many non-pyramidal cells, especially nonfast-spiking ones, were depolarized by NA and FNE through alpha-adrenergic receptors.

findings.title: An inhibitory pull-push circuit in frontal cortex
findings.claim: During arousal, frontal cortex VIP interneurons rapidly inhibit pyramidal neurons directly while also disinhibiting them via a parallel inhibitory pathway, generating a feed-back "pull-push" inhibitory motif in PFC.
findings.claim_source_sentence: During arousal, VIP cells rapidly and directly inhibit pyramidal neurons; VIP cells also indirectly excite these pyramidal neurons via parallel disinhibition.
findings.effect_size: Optogenetic VIP activation produced both direct inhibition (~−1 mV peak) and disinhibition phases on PFC pyramidal cells.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging and optogenetics of VIP and pyramidal neurons in awake mouse mPFC during arousal transitions.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP cells also indirectly excite these pyramidal neurons via parallel disinhibition.

findings.title: Pupil fluctuations track fast switching of cortical states during quiet wakefulness
findings.claim: Slow pupil dilations during quiet wakefulness in mouse cortex track desynchronization, enhanced sensory responses and cell-type-specific recruitment of inhibitory interneurons (including VIP).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Specific subtypes of cortical interneurons were differentially activated during dilation and constriction, consistent with their participation in the observed state changes.
findings.effect_size: Pupil dilations: increased VIP firing, membrane desynchronization, ~30–50% gain increase in sensory responses; constrictions: increased low-frequency oscillations and population correlations.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging + intracellular recording in mouse V1/A1/S1 across pupil-tracked states.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: During dilation, the intracellular membrane potential was desynchronized, sensory responses were enhanced, and population activity was less correlated.

findings.title: Parallel processing by cortical inhibition enables context-dependent behavior
findings.claim: Active engagement during an auditory recognition task produces cholinergic axon activation that simultaneously depolarizes PV, SST and VIP interneurons in auditory cortex; modeling shows that coincident cholinergic drive of all three subtypes — not VIP-mediated disinhibition alone — is required to reproduce context-dependent suppression of cortical activity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cholinergic modulation was involved in context-switching, with cholinergic axons increasing activity during behavior and directly depolarizing inhibitory cells.
findings.effect_size: Network model only matched data when cholinergic input depolarized PV, SST and VIP simultaneously.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging of cholinergic axons + interneurons + pyramidals; whole-cell recordings; computational modeling.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Network modeling captured these findings, but only when modulation coincidently drove all three interneuron subtypes, ruling out either inhibition or disinhibition alone as sole mechanism for active engagement.

findings.title: Layer-specific modulation of neocortical dendritic inhibition during active wakefulness
findings.claim: During active wakefulness in mouse cortex, neocortical layer-1 interneurons (including VIP-class) and SST interneurons across layers are differentially recruited via cholinergic and other neuromodulatory inputs to dynamically shape dendritic inhibition of pyramidal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Layer-specific interneuron activity Somatostatin-expressing interneurons are an important group of inhibitory neurons in the brain that target and thus control the dendrites of pyramidal cells.
findings.effect_size: Layer-1 interneurons (VIP/CGE-derived) became active during active wakefulness, while deep SST cells were inhibited — opposite to what superficial-only recordings had implied.
findings.evidence: Channelrhodopsin-assisted patching of SOM and other interneurons across layers in awake behaving mice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Muñoz et al. used channelrhodopsin-assisted patching to investigate the spatiotemporal pattern of neocortical dendritic inhibition in vivo. They were able to record the activity of somatostatin-expressing interneurons in all neocortical layers in behaving mice.

findings.title: Nicotine excites VIP interneurons to disinhibit pyramidal neurons in auditory cortex
findings.claim: In mouse auditory cortex, bath nicotine strongly and directly depolarizes VIP interneurons, and chemogenetic VIP silencing prevents nicotine-driven disinhibition of pyramidal neurons, providing a cell-type basis for nicotine-induced enhancement of sensory cortical processing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Bath application of nicotine strongly depolarized and excited VIP neurons, weakly depolarized Pyr neurons, and had no effect on the membrane potential of SOM or PV neurons.
findings.effect_size: VIP depolarized strongly by nicotine; sIPSC frequency increased in Pyr/VIP/SST but not PV cells; chemogenetic VIP silencing eliminated nicotine effects on Pyr.
findings.evidence: In vitro whole-cell recordings of identified Pyr/PV/SST/VIP cells; DREADD silencing of VIP cells.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Using Designer Receptors Exclusively Activated by Designer Drugs (DREADDs), we show that chemogenetic inhibition of VIP neurons prevents nicotine's effects on Pyr neurons.

findings.title: Prefrontal cortical ChAT-VIP interneurons provide local excitation by cholinergic synaptic transmission and control attention
findings.claim: A ~0.5–1% subpopulation of cortical VIP interneurons co-expresses choline acetyltransferase (ChAT-VIP) and locally releases acetylcholine that directly excites neighboring pyramidal and inhibitory neurons via fast cholinergic synaptic transmission, controlling attention behavior independently of basal-forebrain ACh.
findings.claim_source_sentence: ChAT-VIP neurons directly excite neighbouring neurons in several layers through fast synaptic transmission of acetylcholine (ACh) in rodent medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC).
findings.effect_size: 10–20% of postsynaptic neurons co-received both ACh and GABA from ChAT-VIP cells; ChAT-VIP silencing impaired attention performance.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic stimulation in ChAT-Cre × VIP-Cre mice + paired recordings + behavior (5-CSRTT attention task).
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: A fraction (10–20%) of postsynaptic neurons that received cholinergic input from ChAT-VIP interneurons also received GABAergic input from these neurons.

findings.title: Cortical ChAT<sup>+</sup> neurons co-transmit acetylcholine and GABA in a target- and brain-region-specific manner
findings.claim: Cortical ChAT+ neurons are nearly all VIP+ and release GABA broadly onto inhibitory cells while sparsely co-releasing acetylcholine onto layer-1 interneurons and other VIP/ChAT cells, with target-specific use of nicotinic vs. GABA-A signaling.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that the nearly all cortical ChAT+ neurons in mice are specialized VIP+ interneurons that release GABA strongly onto other inhibitory interneurons and acetylcholine sparsely onto layer 1 interneurons and other VIP+/ChAT+ interneurons.
findings.effect_size: Only a subset of ChAT/VIP terminals contained vesicular ACh transporter; most postsynaptic responses on inhibitory cells were GABAergic.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of cortical ChAT+ neurons in mice + paired recordings + RNAscope quantification of cholinergic terminals.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: This differential transmission of ACh and GABA based on the postsynaptic target neuron is reflected in VIP+/ChAT+ interneuron pre-synaptic terminals, as quantitative molecular analysis shows that only a subset of these are specialized to release acetylcholine.

findings.title: Barrel cortex VIP/ChAT interneurons suppress sensory responses in vivo
findings.claim: In mouse barrel cortex VIP/ChAT interneurons (~0.5% of neurons) fire faithfully to whisker stimuli and, despite their rarity, optogenetic activation suppresses sensory responses of L2/3 excitatory neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Optogenetic activation of VChIs in the barrel cortex of awake mice suppresses the sensory responses of excitatory neurons in intermediate amplitudes of whisker deflections while increasing the evoked spike latency.
findings.effect_size: VIP/ChAT cells responded with higher fidelity to whisker stimuli than nearby L2/3 pyramidal cells; optogenetic activation suppressed sensory responses.
findings.evidence: In vivo electrophysiology + monosynaptic rabies tracing + optogenetics.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: They responded with higher fidelity to whisker stimulation than the neighboring layer 2/3 pyramidal neurons (Pyrs).

findings.title: Prolonged disynaptic inhibition in the cortex mediated by slow, non-α7 nicotinic excitation of a specific subset of cortical interneurons
findings.claim: Basal-forebrain cholinergic axons activate a specific subset of cortical interneurons (CGE-derived/VIP-class) via slow, non-α7 nicotinic receptors, generating prolonged disynaptic inhibition that reshapes spatiotemporal cortical activity patterns.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cholinergic axons from the basal forebrain of mice excite a specific subset of cortical interneurons via a remarkably slow, non-α7 nicotinic receptor-mediated conductance.
findings.effect_size: Cholinergic-recruited interneurons produced disynaptic IPSCs lasting hundreds of ms in neighboring pyramidals.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of basal-forebrain cholinergic axons + whole-cell recordings in mouse neocortical slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: These inhibitory cells generate a delayed and prolonged wave of disynaptic inhibition in neighboring cortical neurons, altering the spatiotemporal pattern of inhibition in cortical circuits.

findings.title: Corelease of acetylcholine and GABA from cholinergic forebrain neurons
findings.claim: Mouse basal-forebrain cholinergic neurons co-release GABA with acetylcholine onto cortical layer-1 interneurons; conditional deletion of the vesicular GABA transporter from cholinergic neurons removes the GABAergic component, demonstrating direct corelease.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We observed PSCs mediated by GABAA receptors in addition to nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.
findings.effect_size: PSC kinetics and pharmacology consistent with monosynaptic ACh and GABA; Slc32a1 deletion eliminated GABA component.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of ChAT+ neurons + whole-cell recordings of L1 interneurons in mouse cortical slices; floxed Slc32a1 knockout.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We confirmed the direct release of GABA by knocking out Slc32a1 from cholinergic neurons.

findings.title: Nicotine reverses hypofrontality in animal models of addiction and schizophrenia
findings.claim: Mice carrying the human α5-nAChR rs16969968 risk SNP or α5 knockout show reduced VIP-interneuron activity and disinhibition of SST → pyramidal in PFC, producing hypofrontality and behavioural deficits that chronic nicotine reverses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In α5-SNP-expressing and α5-knockout mice, lower activity of vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) interneurons resulted in an increased somatostatin (SOM) interneuron inhibitory drive over layer II/III pyramidal neurons.
findings.effect_size: Chronic nicotine restored VIP activity and reversed PFC hypofrontality.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging in awake α5-SNP/α5-KO mice + behavioral tests + chronic nicotine.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Chronic nicotine administration reversed this hypofrontality, suggesting that administration of nicotine may represent a therapeutic strategy for the treatment of schizophrenia.

findings.title: Central Cholinergic Neurons Are Rapidly Recruited by Reinforcement Feedback
findings.claim: Optogenetically identified basal-forebrain cholinergic neurons broadcast a fast (~18 ms), precisely timed reinforcement signal to cortex that scales with the unexpectedness of reward and punishment, providing a neuromodulatory drive that recruits VIP interneurons cortex-wide.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cholinergic neurons responded to reward and punishment with unusual speed and precision (18±3ms).
findings.effect_size: Cholinergic responses scaled with reinforcement unexpectedness; latency 18±3 ms across nuclei.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic identification + extracellular recordings in basal forebrain of mice performing auditory detection tasks.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Cholinergic responses were scaled by the unexpectedness of reinforcement and were highly similar across neurons and two nuclei innervating distinct cortical areas.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific modulation of neocortical activity by basal forebrain input
findings.claim: Basal forebrain cholinergic input modulates neocortical activity in a cell-type-specific manner: it preferentially recruits non-pyramidal interneuron subtypes (including VIP) and changes cortical state, providing the canonical framework for cholinergic disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cell-type-specific modulation of neocortical activity by basal forebrain input.
findings.effect_size: Different interneuron classes show distinct sensitivities to nicotinic vs. muscarinic activation.
findings.evidence: Review of patch-clamp + in vivo recordings characterizing BF→cortex action.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Cholinergic neurons of the basal forebrain target specific subsets of cortical interneurons that in turn shape cortical activity in a cell-type-specific manner.

findings.title: Neuromodulatory control of localized dendritic spiking in critical period cortex
findings.claim: During the binocular critical period of mouse V1, basal-forebrain acetylcholine released during arousal directly excites SST interneurons (not VIP) to produce localized dendritic spiking and disinhibition needed for binocular plasticity; this cholinergic sensitivity is lost in adulthood.
findings.claim_source_sentence: At the peak of the critical period for binocular plasticity, acetylcholine released from the basal forebrain during periods of heightened arousal directly excites somatostatin-expressing (SST) interneurons.
findings.effect_size: In adults SST cells lost cholinergic sensitivity; binocular receptive fields failed to develop when SST cells were silenced.
findings.evidence: In vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging + slice electrophysiology + optogenetics in critical-period vs. adult mouse V1.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: By adulthood, this cholinergic sensitivity is lost, and compartmentalized dendritic responses are absent but can be re-instated by optogenetic activation of SST cells.

findings.title: Cholinergic and noradrenergic axonal activity contains a behavioral-state signal that is coordinated across the dorsal cortex
findings.claim: Cholinergic basal-forebrain and noradrenergic locus-coeruleus axons across mouse dorsal cortex carry a globally coordinated arousal/movement signal (pupil, whisking, locomotion), so VIP interneurons that integrate ACh+NE inputs receive a brain-state signal coordinated up to 4 mm apart.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Both basal forebrain cholinergic and locus coeruleus noradrenergic neurons track arousal, indexed as pupil diameter, and changes in behavioral engagement, as reflected by bouts of whisker movement and/or locomotion.
findings.effect_size: Both axon populations were broadly synchronized at distances up to 4 mm.
findings.evidence: Mesoscale 2-photon imaging of GCaMP6s in BF cholinergic and LC noradrenergic axons in awake mice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The broad coordination in activity between even distant axonal segments indicates that both of these systems can communicate, in part, through a global signal, especially in relation to changes in behavioral state.

findings.title: Rapid Neuromodulation of Layer 1 Interneurons in Human Neocortex
findings.claim: In human neocortex, all layer-1 GABAergic interneurons (the human homologs of CGE-derived rodent VIP-class L1 cells) show pronounced nicotinic recruitment, while only a small subset co-express ionotropic 5-HT3 receptors — demonstrating cross-species conservation of rapid cholinergic neuromodulation of L1.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our results indicate pronounced nicotinic recruitment of all L1-INs, whereas only a small subset co-expresses the ionotropic HTR3 receptor.
findings.effect_size: Robust α4β2-type nicotinic responses in human L1; minor 5-HT3 responses contrasted with rodent VIP-class.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell recordings of human surgical cortical L1 interneurons + agonist application + transgenic mouse comparison + RNA in situ.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Two comparable physiologically and genetically distinct L1-IN types in both species, together indicating conserved rapid neuromodulation of human neocortical circuits through layer 1.

findings.title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
findings.claim: Locomotion in mouse V1 increases activity of VIP, SST and PV interneurons during visual stimulation, with context-dependent (visual vs. dark) responsiveness; this contests a pure VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibition model and instead implicates parallel state recruitment of all interneuron classes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Locomotion increases the activity of vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP), somatostatin (SST) and parvalbumin (PV)-positive interneurons during visual stimulation, challenging the disinhibition model.
findings.effect_size: Most VIP/PV remained locomotion-responsive in darkness; SST cells lost responsiveness in darkness.
findings.evidence: Two-photon Ca2+ imaging of L2/3 and L4 interneurons in V1 during running.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In darkness, while most VIP and PV neurons remained locomotion responsive, SST and excitatory neurons were largely non-responsive.

findings.title: Vision and Locomotion Shape the Interactions between Neuron Types in Mouse Visual Cortex
findings.claim: A recurrent network model of mouse V1 captures the locomotion-driven gain change only when locomotion increases feedforward synaptic weights and modulates recurrent weights between VIP/SST/PV/Pyr; pure disinhibition fails when visual stimuli are present.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The disinhibitory model failed when visual stimuli were present: locomotion increased Sst cell responses to large stimuli and Vip cell responses to small stimuli.
findings.effect_size: Locomotion required scaling of synaptic weights to reproduce VIP/SST stimulus-dependent responses.
findings.evidence: Two-photon Ca2+ imaging of all four cell classes (Pyr/SST/VIP/PV) + recurrent network model.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Capturing the effects of locomotion, however, required allowing it to increase feedforward synaptic weights and modulate recurrent weights.

findings.title: Cortex-wide response mode of VIP-expressing inhibitory neurons by reward and punishment
findings.claim: VIP interneurons across virtually the entire mouse dorsal cortex are rapidly co-activated by reward and punishment during early auditory-discrimination learning, with response strength only partly explained by arousal — a global cell-type-specific neuromodulator-broadcast mode for VIP cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Reward and punishment during initial learning produce rapid, cortex-wide activation of most VIP interneurons.
findings.effect_size: Reinforcer-evoked activation of VIP cells observed cortex-wide; not predicted by sensory tuning or arousal alone.
findings.evidence: Three-dimensional random-access two-photon microscopy + fiber photometry in dozens of cortical regions during auditory learning.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Neither the weak sensory tuning of VIP interneurons in visual cortex nor their arousal state modulation was fully predictive of reinforcer responses.

findings.title: Global and subtype-specific modulation of cortical inhibitory neurons regulated by acetylcholine during motor learning
findings.claim: Cortex-wide imaging during motor learning shows that cholinergic basal-forebrain inputs drive subtype-specific activation of VIP-INs (initially strong) and SOM-INs (initially weak), and inhibiting VIP-INs increases SOM-IN activity and impairs initial motor learning.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The initial learning phase involved strong activation of vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing INs (VIP-INs) and weak activation of somatostatin-expressing INs (SOM-INs).
findings.effect_size: VIP-IN inhibition increased SST-IN activity and reduced learning rate; cholinergic manipulation altered IN balance.
findings.evidence: Two-photon mesoscale + microscale Ca2+ imaging + cell-type-specific manipulation in mice learning lever-press.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Inactivating VIP-INs increased SOM-IN activity and impaired initial learning.

findings.title: Cell-Type-Specific D1 Dopamine Receptor Modulation of Projection Neurons and Interneurons in the Prefrontal Cortex
findings.claim: In mouse prelimbic PFC, D1 dopamine receptors are enriched in superficial-layer VIP interneurons that co-express calretinin, and D1 agonists strongly enhance firing of these VIP+ cells while sparing PV and SOM cells, implicating VIP cells as cellular substrates of dopaminergic disinhibition in PFC.
findings.claim_source_sentence: D1 receptors are also present in interneurons and enriched in superficial layer VIP-positive (VIP+) interneurons that coexpresses calretinin but absent from parvalbumin-positive (PV+) and somatostatin-positive (SOM+) interneurons.
findings.effect_size: D1 agonist enhanced action-potential firing in D1+ VIP cells; absent in PV/SOM cells.
findings.evidence: D1-Cre/Drd1a-tdTomato + slice electrophysiology and immunohistochemistry in mouse prelimbic PFC.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: D1 receptors strongly and selectively enhance action potential firing in only a subset of these corticocortical neurons and VIP+ interneurons.

findings.title: High Sensitivity Mapping of Cortical Dopamine D2 Receptor Expressing Neurons
findings.claim: High-sensitivity mapping of cortical D2-receptor (Drd2) expression shows that beyond PFC pyramidal/PV cells, Drd2 is also expressed in cortical VIP-class interneurons and several other GABAergic subtypes throughout the cortex, providing a cellular substrate for D2-mediated dopaminergic modulation of disinhibitory circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: D2 dopamine receptor (Drd2) have mostly been examined in the context of cognitive function regulation and neurotransmission modulation of medial prefrontal cortex by principal neurons and parvalbumin positive, fast-spiking, interneurons.
findings.effect_size: Drd2+ cells included VIP-, calretinin- and SST-class interneurons across multiple cortical areas.
findings.evidence: BAC-Drd2-EGFP + RNAscope cell-type mapping across mouse cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Drd2-expressing neurons identified in many cortical areas across all layers, in pyramidal neurons and several interneuron subtypes including VIP+ neurons.

findings.title: Cortical parvalbumin and somatostatin GABA neurons express distinct endogenous modulators of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors
findings.claim: Cortical PV and SOM (but not VIP) GABA neurons selectively express endogenous nicotinic-receptor modulators (lynx2/Lynx1/Slurp/SST-related peptides) that bias the cell-type-specific cholinergic recruitment, contributing to VIP cells' preferential nicotinic responsiveness vs PV/SST.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cortical parvalbumin and somatostatin GABA neurons express distinct endogenous modulators of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.
findings.effect_size: Lynx1 and other nAChR-modulating Ly6 family members were enriched in PV/SOM but not VIP cells.
findings.evidence: Cell-type-specific RNA-seq + in situ hybridization in mouse cortex; functional validation with patch.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: PV neurons and SST neurons express distinct sets of endogenous prototoxin family members that modulate nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.

findings.title: An acetylcholine-activated microcircuit drives temporal dynamics of cortical activity
findings.claim: Intracortical cholinergic input recruits SST interneurons to drive cortical desynchronization and decorrelation; selective optogenetic SST inhibition (but not VIP inhibition) blocks the desynchronization, identifying SST — not VIP — as the necessary cholinergic node for state change.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Selective optogenetic inhibition of SOM responses blocks desynchronization and decorrelation, demonstrating that direct cholinergic activation of SOM neurons is necessary for this phenomenon.
findings.effect_size: SST silencing blocked desynchronization; VIP silencing did not.
findings.evidence: In vivo Vm recordings + optogenetic silencing of SST and VIP cells during cholinergic axon stimulation in mouse V1.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Optogenetic inhibition of vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing neurons does not block desynchronization, despite these neurons being activated at high levels of cholinergic drive.

findings.title: Vasoactive Intestinal Polypeptide-Expressing Interneurons in the Hippocampus Support Goal-Oriented Spatial Learning
findings.claim: Hippocampal CA1 VIP interneurons form functional subpopulations whose activity is shaped by behavioral states and task demands, and optogenetic manipulation shows VIP disinhibition is necessary for goal-oriented spatial learning — extending neuromodulator-driven VIP recruitment beyond cortex to hippocampus.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP+ INs in hippocampal area CA1 form functional subpopulations defined by their modulation by behavioral states and task demands.
findings.effect_size: VIP silencing impaired goal-directed learning and altered place-cell representations.
findings.evidence: Chronic two-photon Ca2+ imaging + optogenetics in CA1 of mice during random foraging vs. goal-oriented tasks.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Optogenetic manipulations of VIP+ INs and computational modeling further showed that VIP+ disinhibition is necessary for goal-directed learning and related reorganization of hippocampal pyramidal cell population dynamics.

findings.title: Neuronal networks and mediators of cortical neurovascular coupling responses in normal and altered brain states
findings.claim: Distinct neuromodulatory pathways (basal forebrain ACh, locus coeruleus NE) recruit highly specific cortical VIP/NOS/CCK interneuron subtypes that mediate region- and state-dependent neurovascular coupling, providing a mechanistic basis by which neuromodulator state alters BOLD signals.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The neural circuits recruited by whisker glutamatergic-, basal forebrain cholinergic- or locus coeruleus noradrenergic pathway stimulation were found to be highly specific and discriminative.
findings.effect_size: VIP/NOS+ interneurons mediated cholinergic-evoked vasodilation; SST cells gave NPY/SST-mediated vasoconstriction.
findings.evidence: Review integrating slice and in vivo work on cortical interneuron + neuromodulatory pathway circuits.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Using the whisker-to-barrel pathway as a model of NVC, we can interrogate the reliability of NVC under enhanced cholinergic or noradrenergic modulation of cortical circuits.

findings.title: Cortical GABA interneurons in neurovascular coupling: relays for subcortical vasoactive pathways
findings.claim: Single VIP-expressing cortical interneurons are sufficient to dilate neighbouring microvessels via VIP-peptide release, and they receive direct cholinergic and serotonergic afferents — establishing VIP cells as neuromodulator-driven mediators of cortical neurovascular coupling.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The evoked firing of single interneurons in whole-cell recordings was sufficient to either dilate or constrict neighboring microvessels.
findings.effect_size: VIP/NOS interneurons → vasodilation; SOM/NPY → vasoconstriction; ACh and 5-HT pathways contacted these vasomotor cells.
findings.evidence: Patch-clamp + RT-mPCR + biocytin filling + immunohistochemistry of cortical interneurons in rat slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Identification of vasomotor interneurons by single-cell RT-mPCR revealed expression of vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) or nitric oxide synthase (NOS) in interneurons inducing dilatation and somatostatin (SOM) in those eliciting contraction.

findings.title: Pharmacology and functions of receptors for vasoactive intestinal peptide and pituitary adenylate cyclase-activating polypeptide: IUPHAR review 1
findings.claim: VIP and PACAP signal through three class-B GPCRs (PAC1, VPAC1, VPAC2) that regulate diverse CNS functions including circadian rhythms, learning and stress responses, providing the receptor pharmacology underlying VIP-peptide signalling in cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP and PACAP exert their actions through three GPCRs – PAC1, VPAC1 and VPAC2 – belonging to class B GPCRs.
findings.effect_size: PAC1 selective for PACAP; VPAC1/2 bind VIP and PACAP with high affinity (low nM).
findings.evidence: IUPHAR receptor nomenclature review.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: PAC1 receptors are selective for PACAP, whereas VPAC1 and VPAC2 respond to both VIP and PACAP with high affinity.

findings.title: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons are impaired in a mouse model of Dravet syndrome
findings.claim: In a mouse model of Dravet syndrome (Scn1a+/−) the irregular-spiking subtype of VIP interneurons shows impaired excitability that is rescued by Nav1.1-specific toxin Hm1a; the IS-vs-CA firing pattern of VIP cells is set by KCNQ M-current and switches to tonic firing on muscarinic receptor activation, demonstrating peptidergic/muscarinic modulation of VIP firing in disease-relevant circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: IS VIP-INs switched to tonic firing with both pharmacologic blockade of M-current and muscarinic acetylcholine receptor activation.
findings.effect_size: Hm1a rescued IS firing; muscarinic activation converted IS to CA firing.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell recordings in Scn1a+/− vs. WT slices + pharmacology.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Application of the Nav1.1-specific toxin Hm1a rescued the observed deficits.

findings.title: Inhibitory circuit gating of auditory critical-period plasticity
findings.claim: In auditory cortex L1 interneurons (a CGE-derived/VIP-related population) gate the tonotopic critical period via cholinergic (nicotinic) input; developmental upregulation of the nicotinic-modulator Lynx1 closes the period, and overriding Lynx1 keeps it open — providing a molecular handle by which neuromodulation controls plasticity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Developmental transitions of nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) sensitivity in these cells by the Lynx1 protein can be overridden to extend critical period closure.
findings.effect_size: Silencing L1 cells abolished critical-period plasticity; Lynx1 overexpression closed it; loss of Lynx1 kept it open.
findings.evidence: Optogenetics + cell-type-specific silencing + Lynx1 manipulation in mouse A1.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Silencing L1 (but not VIP) cells abolishes map plasticity during the tonotopic critical period.

findings.title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
findings.claim: Mediodorsal thalamus selectively engages VIP+ interneurons in PFC layer 1b (while ventromedial thalamus engages NDNF cells in L1a), providing the first cell-type-specific thalamic input route by which cognitive/arousal-related thalamic neuromodulation recruits VIP cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VM engages NDNF+ cells in L1a, and MD drives VIP+ cells in L1b.
findings.effect_size: MD inputs disynaptically inhibited PV cells via VIP-IN recruitment; NDNF cells suppressed L5 dendritic spikes.
findings.evidence: Anatomical tracing + optogenetics + 2-photon Ca2+ imaging in mouse PFC.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: These separate populations of L1 interneurons participate in different inhibitory networks in superficial layers by targeting either PV+ or SOM+ interneurons.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific recruitment of GABAergic interneurons in the primary somatosensory cortex by long-range inputs
findings.claim: Long-range cortical inputs from primary motor cortex preferentially engage VIP interneurons in S1, providing a route by which top-down/motor neuromodulator-state signals (carrying ACh and other neuromodulator timing) bias VIP recruitment, vs. somatosensory cortex inputs that bias PV cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Inputs from whisker-related primary motor cortex are biased to vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-positive GABAergic neurons and potentially result in VIP neuron-mediated disinhibition.
findings.effect_size: Motor → VIP synaptic strengths exceeded those onto PV/SST; somatosensory → PV cells dominated.
findings.evidence: Channelrhodopsin-assisted circuit mapping (CRACM) of long-range inputs to S1 GABAergic subtypes in mice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Projections from other somatosensory cortices strongly recruit parvalbumin (PV)-positive GABAergic neurons and lead to PV neuron-mediated feedforward inhibition of pyramidal neurons in S1.

findings.title: Acetylcholine as a neuromodulator: cholinergic signaling shapes nervous system function and behavior
findings.claim: Acetylcholine acts as a neuromodulator throughout the brain via diverse cell-type-specific muscarinic and nicotinic receptors, biasing networks toward adaptive behaviors — the framework underlying VIP cells' definitional role as a fast nicotinic relay of cortical ACh state.
findings.claim_source_sentence: A common theme is that acetylcholine potentiates behaviors that are adaptive to environmental stimuli and decreases responses to ongoing stimuli that do not require immediate action.
findings.effect_size: ACh effects depend strongly on receptor subtype (M1–M5; α2-α7, β2-β4 nAChR) and target neuronal population (PV, SST, VIP).
findings.evidence: Comprehensive review of cellular, synaptic and behavioral effects of cholinergic signaling.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The diverse effects of acetylcholine depend on the site of release, the receptor subtypes, and the target neuronal population.

argument_groups.argument: In Vivo VIP Activity and Behavioral Function
